heading: Seventeen Seventy vs Severnlea — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Seventeen Seventy, Queensland and Severnlea, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Seventeen Seventy has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Severnlea (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Seventeen Seventy or Severnlea safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Seventeen Seventy has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Severnlea scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Seventeen Seventy scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Severnlea scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Seventeen Seventy or Severnlea?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Seventeen Seventy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
